title: Web Application Attack
briefing: The web server's access log is generating attack alerts. A single external source is probing the application with multiple payload types. Determine what the attacker is attempting, whether any request succeeded, and how to respond.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> rule.groups: web OR rule.id: 31106. Inspect data.url for the payloads. Note the HTTP status code in the alert (200 = the app returned success).

questions.prompt: Source IP(s) of the web attack?
questions.explain: A single external IP issued every malicious request in this scenario.
SQL injection, XSS, path traversal and command injection are all web-application attacks.

questions.prompt: Which payload types are present in the requests? (select all)
The alerts fired on rule 31106 ('web attack returned code 200'). What does the 200 tell you?
options: The server returned success to the attacker's request
The request never reached the server
questions.answer: The server returned success to the attacker's request
questions.explain: HTTP 200 means the app served the malicious request without rejecting it — worth confirming impact urgently.
agent.name on the web alerts is app01.

options: Put a WAF rule / virtual patch in front of the app
Review the app + DB for signs of compromise
Rotate any credentials the app exposes
answer: Put a WAF rule / virtual patch in front of the app
Review the app + DB for signs of compromise
Rotate any credentials the app exposes
questions.explain: Because requests returned 200, treat as possible compromise: block, virtual-patch, hunt, rotate.
